response: The internet began as a US Department of Defense networking project in the 1960s, with ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969 by sending a message from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. A major turning point came in 1983 with the adoption of TCP/IP, followed by Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 and the launch of the Mosaic browser in 1993, which helped bring the web to ordinary users. The text argues that the internet evolved through key waves of growth—including the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, the rise of social media in the mid-2000s, smartphones after the iPhone’s 2007 launch, and cloud computing with AWS in 2006. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and forms the backbone of modern commerce, communication, and entertainment.
